tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent at $121.40/bbl on Mar 22 (Day 20 close +7.6%) — Saudi Arabia launched airstrikes on IRGC naval and missile facilities at Bandar Abbas on Mar 20 (Reuters), first direct Saudi military action against Iran. Iran retaliated with 15 missiles toward Abqaiq on Mar 22; 3 struck perimeter, no production impact (Al Jazeera, Reuters). Tail risk at 40%; working range $120-140 in stress, $160+ in tail. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta; add on any dip toward $115.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent at $121.40 on Mar 22; ASEAN Energy Equities est. +5.5%. ASEAN Banks under acute pressure as oil-importer inflation and credit spread widening accelerate. Saudi airstrikes on Bandar Abbas on Mar 20 have moved the conflict into a Saudi-Iran bilateral exchange phase — the 'defined bilateral exchange' framework has fully collapsed. Conviction in energy-over-banks pair remains high. Trim only if Brent breaks below $105 on confirmed ceasefire and P&I cover restoration announcement.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Renewed Oil Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP est. 61.20 (Mar 22, risk-off). Brent at $121.40 and new JKM Platts assessment at $23.40/MMBtu — Philippines LNG import costs surging materially. Saudi airstrikes on Bandar Abbas on Mar 20 and Iran's Abqaiq missile strike on Mar 22 eliminate any near-term ceasefire. PHP 61.00 breached; next stress level 62.00-63.50. Tail risk at 40% if Iran lands a damaging strike on Saudi oil infrastructure. Stop only if Saudi Arabia and Iran reach bilateral de-escalation pact.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Oil Import Stress Back in Play
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR est. 17,650 on Mar 22 — breaking through 17,500 as Brent surges to $121.40. Saudi airstrikes on IRGC facilities at Bandar Abbas on Mar 20; Iran's Abqaiq missile strike on Mar 22 — Indonesia's oil-import bill has jumped materially again. ID 10Y est. 7.05%; Bank Indonesia in full intervention mode. IDR stress target 18,000 is now in reach; tail above 18,500 if Abqaiq takes production damage. Maintain and reinforce the short-IDR position.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG est. ~185bp on Mar 22 (+13bp); ASEAN HY est. ~555bp (+25bp). Saudi airstrikes on Bandar Abbas on Mar 20 and Iran's Abqaiq missile strike on Mar 22 — Saudi-Iran direct exchange is now live. 200bp is the next stress target on iTraxx Asia IG; 600bp on ASEAN HY. Add to CDS protection; trim only if Brent breaks below $105 on confirmed ceasefire and P&I cover restoration is announced.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Add Size on Confirmed Assessment
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $23.40/MMBtu (new Platts assessment post Qatar LNG hub attack, Reuters/Platts). TTF est. ~€74.20/MWh (est., Day 20). Iran's attack on Qatar's Ras Laffan hub on Mar 19 ended the partial restart narrative; Saudi airstrikes on IRGC on Mar 20 reinstate acute supply shock. US LNG at capacity; Australian LNG is the only alternative. JKM-over-TTF long conviction is high — add size on any confirmation that Ras Laffan damage assessment extends the outage beyond 4 weeks.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's refinancing window is fully closed. Brent at $121.40, iTraxx Asia IG est. ~185bp, ASEAN HY est. ~555bp — unsecured market inaccessible. Saudi airstrikes on IRGC at Bandar Abbas on Mar 20; Iran's Abqaiq missile strike on Mar 22 — risk premium extends indefinitely. Activate secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures now. Every day of delay raises execution risk.

PTTEP should pre-fund before THB deteriorates further — TH 10Y est. 2.18%, USD/THB est. 33.80 on Mar 22. Brent at $121.40 boosts upstream EBITDA but Saudi-Iran direct exchange and Iran's Abqaiq missile strike on Mar 22 spike Thailand's gas import costs to $23.40/MMBtu. Local-duration window is closing as BOT easing probability reaches zero. Execute H2 2026 prefunding and run a sanctions/compliance review on any Gulf JV exposure immediately.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity immediately. Brent $121.40 and JKM $23.40 (new Platts assessment) spike Wilmar's feedstock and bunker costs materially above prior estimates. USD/SGD est. 1.2960. Saudi Arabia's direct military exchange with Iran on Mar 20-22 adds catastrophic freight-route risk. The IG bank market window is still open but narrowing rapidly — execute before further Saudi-Iran escalation re-prices trade-finance availability.

Hyflux must activate restructuring immediately. Base probability 8%, stress 52%, tail 40%; Brent $121.40; ASEAN HY est. ~555bp. Saudi-Iran direct military exchange began Mar 20; Iran's Abqaiq missile strike on Mar 22 — gas-fired power costs are spiking into desalination economics via JKM $23.40. Any market normalisation is beyond H2 2026. Unsecured market is effectively closed; pursue private-credit and quasi-sovereign structures with maximum urgency.
